summary: Excellent matrix recording captures a high‑temp, jazzy Help > Slip sequence and monster Franklin’s Tower with meaty, balanced bass.
review: The three reviewers rave about the show’s soaring energy – a “high‑temp” Help on the Way > Slipknot! segue that feels rich and jazzy, and a “monster” Franklin’s Tower that delivers pure joy. Recording quality is described as outstanding, with crisp boards blended into an audience matrix that preserves both clarity and atmosphere.

ai_rating.rationale: All three reviews are enthusiastic (5‑star) and highlight both stellar playing and excellent sound, though only three reviews limit confidence to medium.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Reviewers note meaty, balanced bass, crisp clarity, and an outstanding blend of board and audience sources; no major flaws mentioned.

show_quality.setlist_flow: High‑energy performance with a hot, jazzy Help>Slip transition that launches into an explosive Franklin’s Tower, creating a seamless and exhilarating flow.

band_member_comments.Phil: His bass tone is praised as meaty, balanced and prominently featured in this period.
